what_it_does: Stores a noticeboard message in the dragnet-development channel, recording a feature request from tim asking whether updates can appear live on screen without requiring a manual reload.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, board:// address, type=message, source=claude_code, state=sent, author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development
created and updated timestamps are identical
history contains a single 'filed' state transition
Body is an informal, typo-laden feature request: 'if you make an updated updates live on my screen rather than me having to reload if it isn't already'
No thread, no links, no code symbols
standouts: The title and body both contain the duplicated phrase 'updated updates'
The request is for real-time/live UI updates without manual refresh
Filed by tim in the dragnet-development channel
No acceptance criteria or context about which screen/client
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format: YAML frontmatter followed by a Markdown body
Uses a board:// URI address scheme
Tracks state history with from/to/by/ts/note fields
concerns: Body phrasing is ambiguous and contains a typo ('updated updates')
Unclear whether live updates already exist or which part of the UI is meant
No reproduction steps, acceptance criteria, or priority
notes: A brief, typo-laden feature request from tim asking for live UI updates in dragnet-development, captured as a sent noticeboard message.
questions: Does a live-update mechanism already exist for this screen?
What exactly does 'updated updates' refer to?
Which screen or client should receive live updates?
Is this a bug report or a feature request?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose is to record a channel message/request, and it does exactly that: it captures tim's request for live updates.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to request a push/live-update feature so that changes are visible immediately without the user refreshing the screen.
contribution: Captures a user-reported need for live screen updates in dragnet-development, preserving the exact wording, author, timestamp, channel, and sent state for downstream triage.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard message from tim in dragnet-development requesting that updates appear live on screen without requiring a manual reload.
intention_for_embedding: To file a feature request for real-time update visibility in the dragnet-development channel.
